violations.description: Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee observed handling ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Witnessed kitchen worker handling lettuce, buns , and toast with bare hands.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. Containers of gravy, and other food items in the walk in cooler were dated 4/20, 4/28, and 4/30. This exceeds the ten days allowed.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Filters in the hood system need to be cleaned.

Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. The drain lines for the walk in cooler and freezer units run to buckets on the shelf and floor.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. The butter spreads on the microwaves in the kitchen temped at 92 degrees. These spreads had ingredients mixed into them. These need to be treated as a TCS food and kept refrigerated.
Hot holding and cold holding temperatures maintained, unwrapped food not reserved (5 pts deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
